figures.caption: Fig. 2. Overview of the experimental setup. Left: We investigate swarms of simulated robots that explore a bounded environment to discover a hidden target region (not shown). Right: Real-world equivalent of the setup.

Fig. 3. Overview of the system architecture, including user interface SwarmUI as used in Study 2. The participant interacts with the swarm simulation via the user interface. They control one robot of the swarm.
Table 1. LME models of robot swarm parameters on warmth & competence. SE = standard error; t = t-statistic. Adapted from [39].
Table 3. Averaged ratings of warmth and competence in human—robot teamwork.

Fig. 4. Boxplots show the changes in warmth (red) and competence (blue) ratings between Study 1 and Study 2, with each dot representing an individual robot swarm team. Significance levels: *p < 0.05, **p < 0.01, ***p < 0.001.

Fig. 1. Two studies were conducted where participants rated the perceived warmth and competence of swarms of robots. In the first (left), they only observe the swarm. In the second (right), they also control a member of the swarm.
